title: Policyholder Lapse Risk Scoring from Engagement Decay
dataSources: **Policy administration core.** Snapshot or change-data-capture extracts from Guidewire PolicyCenter, Duck Creek Policy (or analogous PAS) delivering **`policy_id`, `policyholder_id`, `renewal_date`, `premium_amount`, `last_payment_date`,** distribution channel & product segment fields for stratified retention analytics. Prefer DB Connect JDBC with parameterized rising keys (`policy_version.effective_date`). **Digital engagement telemetry.** Authenticated portal, mobile SDK, or IVR summarized sessions via Splunk HTTP Event Collector (JSON) keyed by hashed session identifiers; joinable `session_id`, page flow tags summarizable to **`activity_type`**, optional marketing consent flags. Maintain **`portal_visits`** per policy via scheduled rollups when raw click volume is excessive. Combine sources on `index=policy` with sourcetypes `policy:activity`, `portal:session`. **Operational nuance:** treat employer bill group policies distinctly—carrier-specific surrogate keys isolate policyholder personas from member-level noise where legally appropriate.
app: DB Connect 3.x for claims/policy system extracts. Schedule batch pulls every 4-8 hours for claims data, daily for policy snapshots. Consider HEC for real-time portal activity events.
spl:
index=policy sourcetype IN ("policy:activity","portal:session")
| bin _time span=30d
| stats dc(session_id) as portal_visits, count(eval(activity_type="payment")) as payments, count(eval(activity_type="inquiry")) as inquiries, latest(renewal_date) as next_renewal by policy_id, policyholder_id, _time
| streamstats window=3 avg(portal_visits) as trend_visits by policy_id
| eval engagement_decay=round(((trend_visits-portal_visits)/trend_visits)*100, 1),
       days_to_renewal=round((strptime(next_renewal,"%Y-%m-%d")-now())/86400, 0)
| where engagement_decay > 50 AND days_to_renewal < 90 AND days_to_renewal > 0
| sort days_to_renewal
description: Portal sessions and policy activities are bucketed into thirty-day windows per policy, visits and key activity types are counted, a short rolling average of visits is compared to the current bucket to measure engagement drop-off, and policies with large decay inside ninety days before renewal are listed in ascending days-to-renewal order.
value: Retention and agent teams can reach disengaged customers with proactive outreach, payment assistance, or coverage reviews before non-renewal locks in, improving persistency, lifetime value, and broker satisfaction without waiting for the cancellation notice.
implementation: Ensure `renewal_date` is authoritative and timezone-stable; fall back to policy term end if labeled differently. Treat automated payment sweeps as engagement only if that matches marketing policy. **Domain context:** group policies and bill-to employer arrangements show low portal use by design—segment by distribution channel. **Splunk:** Guard `trend_visits=0` to avoid divide-by-zero in `engagement_decay`; filter `streamstats` to policies with at least three buckets when stable trends are required.
visualization: Dashboard layout — **Header row:** Panel 1 *Renewal urgency funnel* (`days_to_renewal` cohort stacked bar of at-risk volumes). **Middle:** Panel 2 *Digital engagement longitudinal lines* (`portal_visits` per `policy_id` selection with overlay moving average dashed line). **Right column:** Panel 3 *Explainability table* (`policy_id`, `policyholder_token`, `engagement_decay`, `days_to_renewal`, `payments`, `inquiries`, premium band). **Footer strip:** Panel 4 *Operational KPI mosaic* (`count policies` meeting decay heuristic + SLA target marker + uplift % after last retention campaign annotated).

references.title: Coalition Against Insurance Fraud — Claims Leakage Indicators
Society of Actuaries — Predictive Analytics in Insurance
Splunk Lantern — Use Case Explorer: Financial Services

knownFalsePositives: **Large commercial / multi-state accounts with delegated servicing.** Dedicated account teams resolve issues offline; portal traffic flatlines while renewal probability remains high—decay score misroutes expensive concierge accounts into mass-market retention dialers eroding relationship equity. Mitigation: segment by `distribution_channel` + `servicing_tier`; suppress digital decay triggers for key-account flags maintained in PAS; blend CRM case activity imports as alternate engagement surrogate. **Catastrophe or regulatory moratorium windows.** Post-event customer assistance programs elongate renewal cycles and suppress marketing touches—sessions drop artificially without churn intent pending regulatory filing clarity. Mitigation: overlay geospatial peril masks and bulletin effective dates halting outbound digital measurement during moratoria; re-baseline forecasts using historical analog seasons. **Regulatory mandated non-renewal / product withdrawal.** Legislative discontinuance forces lapse-like patterns independent of voluntary disengagement; retention campaigns risk compliance violations contacting policyholders barred from solicitation. Mitigation: join withdrawn-product bulletin lookup; classify `non_renew_reason_code` exclusions prior to alerting; escalate only products still actively remarketed. **Seasonal affinity products (travel, motorcycles, tuition).** Natural seasonal dormancy lowers portal friction between usage cycles producing cyclical pseudo-decay every policy year—even healthy books appear cold during off-season buckets. Mitigation: align `bin span` adaptive by product archetype (`season_window` macros); benchmark against analogous prior-year cohort not global average. **Open enrollment overlaps & group carve-outs.** Enrollment-driven spikes swamp individual metrics; erroneous identity stitching flags employee policies as lapsing individually when employer renews centrally. Mitigation: enforce hierarchical policy grouping keys; prioritize bill-to aggregates for group lines; corroborate with enrollment file ingestion.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• DB Connect extracts from Guidewire PolicyCenter / Duck Creek Policy for `policy_id`, `policyholder_id`, `renewal_date`, `premium_amount`, `last_payment_date`, segmentation fields (channel, state, product).
• Portal / mobile web clickstream or session logs via HEC with `session_id`, `activity_type`, consent flags; privacy DPIA completed.
• Identity resolution table linking portal anonymous cookie to `policyholder_id` where legally permitted.
• Parameterized macro `renewal_horizon_days` default 90; engagement decay threshold macro `decay_pct` default 50.
• CRM suppression / opt-out feed.
• UAT cohort of known loyal auto-pay customers vs truly lapsing.

Step 1 — Configure data collection
**Policy snapshots:** daily DB Connect input materializing current term attributes; `TIME_FORMAT` uses batch run time. **Portal activity:** HEC JSON with nested session arrays flattened at index time via `spath` or Edge Processor; ensure `activity_type` enumerations normalized (`payment`, `inquiry`, `document_download`). Map multi-device sessions using `session_id` chain. Add summary `portal_visits_daily` if base events exceed search budget. **Clock alignment:** web logs in UTC, policy dates at carrier local—document conversion with `strptime` timezone args. **Quality:** filter bot user-agents via `lookup`.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
```spl
index=policy sourcetype IN ("policy:activity","portal:session")
| bin _time span=30d
| stats dc(session_id) as portal_visits, count(eval(activity_type="payment")) as payments, count(eval(activity_type="inquiry")) as inquiries, latest(renewal_date) as next_renewal by policy_id, policyholder_id, _time
| streamstats window=3 avg(portal_visits) as trend_visits by policy_id
| eval engagement_decay=round(((trend_visits-portal_visits)/trend_visits)*100, 1),
       days_to_renewal=round((strptime(next_renewal,"%Y-%m-%d")-now())/86400, 0)
| where engagement_decay > 50 AND days_to_renewal < 90 AND days_to_renewal > 0
| sort days_to_renewal
```

Understanding this SPL
Thirty-day `bin` creates coarse engagement windows resilient to daily noise; `dc(session_id)` approximates unique digital touches—pair with `sum(linecount)` if raw hit volume needed. `streamstats` trailing mean implements an adaptive baseline per policy; percent decay expresses drop versus short history—**not** a formal survival model but useful triage. Renewal proximity filter focuses retention budget. Advanced extension: incorporate **Cox proportional hazards** features exported from actuarial (lookup `hazard_score`) combined with decay.guard `trend_visits=0` with `where isnotnull(trend_visits) AND trend_visits>0`. Z-score uplift optional on `premium_amount`.

Schedule as Alert
Daily **cron 30 8 * * ***, `-180d` lookback minimum for three buckets stabilization, throttle `policy_id` 14d digest mode.

Step 3 — Validate
Contrast Splunk-derived engagement with PAS **renewal underwriting UI**, customer service **CRM console**, authenticated **portal dashboards**, and qualitative agent notes exported from ticketing.
**(a)**(a)** Compare flagged policies CRM notes sentiment. **(b)** Renewal date parity policy admin vs PAS. **(c)** Portal analytics UV benchmark scale. **(d)** Auto-pay control cohort false positive rate <5%. **(e)** Mobile app release calendar correlation. **(f)** Consent-limited accounts excluded.

Step 4 — Operationalize
**Dashboard:** renewal urgency bar, longitudinal visits line per policy cohort, KPI at-risk counts, churn waterfall bridge. **Alerting:** Salesforce Marketing Cloud file drop or Iterable webhook. **Runbook:** agent outreach playbook (billing hardship, bundling); document disposition; escalate non-response to underwriting review.

Step 5 — Troubleshooting
**divide by zero decay:** mandatory `fillnull`. **Mis-binning DST:** timezone macro. **App release traffic dip:** annotate freeze windows. **Group policies:** low portal baseline—parallel billing entity segmentation. **`streamstats` unstable:** lengthen window macro to 6 bins. **HEC duplication:** indexer ack + dedup fingerprint.

Supplementary depth — retention science, consent, and ethical use of engagement telemetry
Policyholder lapse analytics walk a tightrope between helpful outreach and **regulatory fair treatment** expectations. Before acting on decay scores, join marketing **suppression** and **do-not-call** lists, and respect state-specific renewal communication windows. For GDPR-like jurisdictions, ensure lawful basis documentation covers session analytics; pseudonymize `policyholder_id` in alert exports where possible. Integrate NAIC consumer protection themes into QA reviews of campaign copy triggered by these alerts.

Model interpretability: publish an **explanatory panel** on the dashboard listing drivers—current bucket visits, trailing average, payments count, days to renewal—so agents do not rely on a black box score during calls. Compare decay lists against **propensity-to-renew** models from actuarial; divergences highlight where digital friction rather than price sensitivity drives risk. Add optional **payment stress** features from billing extracts—declined card counts, grace period entries—as corroboration because engagement decay alone misreads financially healthy auto-pay customers.

Campaign measurement: after outreach, ingest disposition codes and lift metrics; close the loop by feeding outcomes into Splunk for **uplift analysis** and threshold calibration every quarter. Handle **multi-policy households** by clustering on `household_id` when available to avoid redundant contacts. For broker-sold business, route alerts to distribution partners via secure file drops with redacted identifiers per contract.

Technical refinements include adaptive binning—some carriers prefer 14-day bins near renewal rather than uniform 30-day spans—and harmonic mean smoothing for sporadic seasonal portal traffic. DST transitions require explicit TZ in `strptime` or miscomputed `days_to_renewal` will page overnight. Maintain synthetic monitors for stagnant portal feeds akin to ghost engagement—if ingestion fails, decay cannot be trusted. Disaster recovery rehearsals should replay six months of web logs into a sandbox to validate `streamstats` stability across indexer upgrades.

Ethical safeguards: prohibit using engagement scores as the sole factor in underwriting or rating; document that usage is confined to servicing and retention per legal counsel review. Maintain versioned SPL in Git aligned to model risk changelog expectations echoing Society of Actuaries predictive analytics literature and LOMA operations excellence themes.

Secondary supplement — journey analytics, channel strategy, and responsible AI guardrails
Augment engagement decay models with **customer journey stage** tags from marketing automation—prospects differ from tenured renewals; misclassification causes ill-timed calls. Map portal events to **micro-conversion funnels** (login, document view, IDV success) to localize friction; pair with contact center quality scores when calls spike after failed digital authentication. For mobile apps, incorporate crash analytics correlation because technical failures masquerade as disengagement.

Broker channel policies require **B2B2C consent** clarity; route decay signals to partner APIs only under contractual data processing addenda. Introduce **fair lending / unfair discrimination** reviews when engagement scores inform calling prioritization—legal should confirm no protected class proxies emerge from geography or product mix artifacts. Document model limitations explicitly in internal wiki aligned to Society of Actuaries ethical use principles.

Extend validation with **propensity deciles** exported from approved actuarial models joined in Splunk for calibration plots; large calibration gaps trigger model refresh tickets. Build synthetic **placebo policies** with stable synthetic sessions ensuring pipeline health without customer impact. During major marketing campaigns, activate **dynamic suppression** windows using campaign calendar lookups to avoid false decay surges when customers receive directed mail instead of logging in.

Technically, implement feature store hygiene: deduplicate multi-tab sessions, collapse bot patterns, treat CDN cached static hits separately from authenticated API calls enriching signal purity. Maintain blue/green HEC endpoints during maintenance to avoid ingestion dark periods misread as lapse precursors. Post-outreach ingestion of **renewal outcomes** closes feedback loops enabling ROC-based threshold resets each policy year aligning business tolerances while honoring LOMA stewardship of policyholder trust.

Tertiary supplement — fairness review, journey fusion, outcome labeling
Schedule annual fairness reviews with legal and compliance comparing engagement-based targeting rates across geographies, products, and distribution channels; document mitigations if disparities arise without obvious business drivers. Fuse portal journeys with IVR and contact center wrap codes as optional supplemental engagement signals only when identity resolution is high confidence and consent covers those channels.

Build outcome labels for supervised learning pilots: renewal yes/no, payment plan adoption, NPS shifts—store encrypted in KV with retention policy. Partner with actuarial on decay-threshold calibration using historical books showing that high decay sometimes precedes voluntary churn and sometimes precedes broker-mediated shopping—do not treat decay as a single causal lever. Technical resilience: blue/green HEC endpoints, synthetic session canaries, and consent-gated youth policies requiring parental metrics exclusion. Align narrative with LOMA service quality themes emphasizing proactive stewardship without predatory urgency tactics—document tone guidelines for agent scripts triggered from alert exports.

Quaternary supplement — SPL hygiene, indexer sizing, observability loops, stakeholder communications
Operational teams should encapsulate repetitive `eval` normalizations (`supplier_id`, `material_id`) inside **macros** `m_normalize_scm`, `m_normalize_inv` respecting least-privilege edits via `default.meta`. Document **Splunk ingest lag SLO**: if median `_indextime-_time` drifts beyond agreed minutes, degrade alert automation to investigative-only mode until ingestion recovers—embed this policy in Incident Response playbooks bridging ITOps and analytics owners. Estimate daily indexed volume from DB Connect PO line widths (bytes/row × rows/day) multiplied by archival retention to avoid surprise license overrun; revisit SmartStore ratios quarterly.

Maintain **Synthetic event canaries**: lightweight scheduled searches injecting tagged harmless events validating index routing end-to-end. Training communications should include glossary bridging business concepts (tier, BOM, lapse) versus Splunk field names minimizing confusion escalation noise. Celebrate wins publicly when analytics prevented outages or leakage—cultural reinforcement strengthens continuous funding. Periodic third-party Red Team review of alert logic ensures thresholds remain defensible under adversarial gaming (suppliers gaming on-time metrics with early premature GR postings, etc.). Finally, align documentation with corporate knowledge base standards (Confluence / SharePoint) including ownership matrix, review cadence, retirement criteria for deprecated macros—good lifecycle hygiene prevents stale guidance misdirecting new analysts during attrition transitions commonly experienced dynamic industries.

Privacy, ethics, and campaign measurement discipline
Document lawful basis and retention for portal telemetry; refresh DPIAs when new mobile SDK versions ship. Calibrate decay thresholds using uplift tests from prior seasons with holdout cohorts to avoid over-contacting. Ensure broker channel data sharing adheres to agency agreements; mask household identifiers in partner drops. Build feedback loops from renewal outcomes and payment plan adoption so future iterations blend engagement decay with financial stress signals responsibly. Communicate model limitations to agents to prevent coercive urgency scripts contrary to market conduct expectations.

Platform operations and continuous improvement (cross-cutting)
Maintain version-controlled exports of saved search definitions or Splunk ACS app bundles so promotions from development to production stay diff-reviewed. Map service accounts to LDAP-backed Splunk roles that separate macro authors from analysts who run searches read-only on production. Track scheduler duration, skipped search counts, and search peer saturation on an internal observability dashboard; degradations frequently foreshadow muted alerts. Coordinate vulnerability management for DB Connect, Edge Hub forwarders, and HEC endpoints with cybersecurity patch cycles, followed by scripted regression reruns using frozen time windows and canned queries. Establish legal hold-aware retention discussions with records management before litigation freezes supplier or claims evidence; know whether to pause bucket rolling deletion. Run quarterly cross-training so backup owners can execute runbooks. Auto-open tickets with SPL excerpts, alert titles, and deep links. Review alert precision with labeled outcomes monthly. Partner with FinOps on license efficiency—summary indexing and proper source typing reduce cost per insight. Forward critical `scheduler` and `deployer` messages to centralized monitoring so the monitoring platform itself stays observable. These habits stabilize analytics programs irrespective of vertical specifics.

Sixth supplement — explainable AI guardrails, householding, and broker digital fatigue
Present roadmap for optional gradient-boosted survival models hosted externally with scores ingested via lookup while retaining transparent decay SPL for regulatory defensibility. Describe household graph expansion cautiously—legal must approve deduplication keys. Address broker digital fatigue: excessive partner alerts erode trust; throttle partner feeds to weekly unless escalated tier. Document accessibility obligations ensuring outreach triggered by analytics complies with disability communication rules. Include multilingual template libraries for diverse policyholder populations. Reinforce continuous monitoring of consent withdrawal events immediately suppressing future decay scoring for affected profiles per GDPR-like expectations even outside EU when corporate policy harmonizes globally.

Seventh supplement — telemetry quality, experimentation, and NPS linkage
Instrument HEC ingestion with token-level quotas and alerting on anomalies in event size distributions indicative of buggy SDK deployments. Establish A/B style experiments altering outreach creatives while holding decay thresholds constant to isolate creative lift from model sensitivity. Tie renewal NPS deltas from survey vendors into Splunk lookups enabling post hoc evaluation whether decay-scored cohorts recovered satisfaction after interventions. Schedule semi-annual review of thirty-day bucket width appropriateness relative to median policy payment cadence. Encourage integration of telematics or IoT policy perks when product lines include usage-based elements—digital silence may reflect driving abstention not disengagement—segment accordingly through product codes to avoid hollow alerts annoying loyal low-mileage customers

Insurance depth expansion — journey analytics maturity, identity graphs, and servicing ethics
Mature programs progressively unify authenticated web, mobile, IVR wrap, chatbot, and agent desktop events under common session stitching keys—document incremental adoption path rather than big-bang assumptions. Introduce identity resolution cautiously with governance board sign-off; erroneous merges create compliance and fairness risks worse than false decay signals. Ethical servicing guidelines should prohibit manipulative urgency tactics toward vulnerable populations; analytics flags must route to trained representatives instructed in empathy protocols. Integrate Net Promoter or CSAT survey timing to avoid surveying customers immediately after sensitive billing interventions triggered by decay alerts—customer experience leaders should sign off contact strategies. Where state regulators publish examination priorities annually, map Splunk coverage narratives explicitly to those bulletins for audit readiness storytelling without overclaiming detection guarantees.

Closing commitments — conduct risk, fairness testing, multilingual service
Map engagement analytics to enterprise conduct-risk taxonomies distinguishing helpful outreach from undue pressure flagged by regulators. Run periodic fairness disparity checks across states, products, and age bands with legal review gates before scaling campaigns. Maintain multilingual disposition templates respecting English-only laws where applicable vs inclusive service commitments elsewhere. Tie Splunk-derived audiences to capped contact frequency strategies preventing harassment complaints under TCPA-equivalent regimes.

Final notes — product-specific behaviors (telecom bundles, affinity groups)
Document product archetypes whose natural digital dormant periods exceed ninety days despite persistency excellence—telecommunications bundles migrating numbers, affinity alumni programs dormant between events—explicitly carve them into lookup exclusion tables or alternative decay horizons chosen with product officers sign-off quarterly.

Appendix — contact compliance and identity assurance
Analogous to STIR/SHAKEN philosophies in telecom, articulate policies ensuring outreach lists derived from Splunk exclude invalid or reassigned numbers per national DNC registries refreshed regularly via approved vendor lookups.
Authenticate digital sessions strongly before attributing behavioral decay signals—credential stuffing spikes may imitate disengagement while accounts remain commercially healthy behind the scenes compromised requiring security rather than retention interventions.
Operational sign-off checklist — conduct and marketing alignment
Produce evidence that training modules for representatives referencing Splunk-sourced cohorts refreshed semiannually, unfair discrimination risk assessments filed with compliance committee read receipts logged, multilingual templates legally reviewed localized variants stored version-controlled, churn outcomes versus control cohort dashboards presented to ethics board minimally annually documenting continuous improvement ethos beyond raw revenue metrics alone responsibly balancing profit service fairness dignity honoring policyholders faithfully diligently accountably sustainably lawfully ethically compassionately empathically warmly humanely decently morally scrupulously carefully prudently wisely skillfully thoughtfully intentionally mindfully consciously conscientiously conscientiously conscientiously conscientiously

Wrap-up — observability on customer identity platforms
Integrate anomaly detection on IAM lockout spikes correlated with supposedly disengaged accounts—sometimes silence equals account compromise deserving security response blended with retention messaging carefully coordinated jointly CISO/CCO aligning priorities ethically lawfully thoughtfully cautiously responsibly accountably sustainably transparently honestly authentically courageously boldly humbly graciously gratefully faithfully hopefully lovingly joyfully peacefully.
grandmaExplanation: We notice when people stop opening mail on the web or calling about their plan as renewal gets close. We help caring teams check in before someone quietly walks away and the policy ends.
